tools.name: analyze_csharp_code
tools.description: Runs every enabled C# code-quality check on a single source file and returns one combined report. Covers coding style, member ordering, naming, async/await patterns, nullable safety, project structure, and (for test files) test conventions. Use this whenever generating, editing, or reviewing C# code. Pass the source as content and the file's absolute path as originalPath. When checking a test file, also pass the production type's namespace as originalNamespace and the test file path as comparedPath so test-style rules can verify the mirror structure.

content.description: The C# source code to check.

originalPath.description: Absolute path of the file whose source is in content.

originalNamespace.description: Namespace of the type in the original file (e.g. 'MyApp.Services').

comparedPath.description: Absolute path of the test file being compared against the original (used by test-style rules).

tools.name: analyze_nuget_references
tools.description: Inspects a .csproj file's PackageReference entries for hygiene issues: duplicate references, floating or wildcard versions (e.g. '*' or version ranges), missing Version attributes (unless Central Package Management is enabled via ManagePackageVersionsCentrally), and packages that have a built-in .NET equivalent. Use this whenever adding, updating, or reviewing NuGet dependencies. Pass the full .csproj XML as content.

content.description: The full XML content of the .csproj file to inspect.

tools.name: analyze_git_commit_message
tools.description: Validates a Git commit message against project conventions. Covers Conventional Commits format (type, scope, subject, line lengths) and content best practices (no anti-patterns in the body). Use this before creating any commit. Pass the full message — subject and body — as content.

content.description: The full commit message to validate.

tools.name: read_editorconfig
tools.description: Resolves the effective .editorconfig properties for a single file by walking up the directory tree, evaluating glob patterns, and applying section cascading. Returns the final flat key/value map that applies to that file. Use this when you need to know the coding-style rules (indent style, charset, end-of-line, language-specific keys) in force at a particular file path. Pass the file's absolute path.

path.description: Absolute path to the file whose effective .editorconfig properties should be resolved.

tools.name: analyze_typescript_code
tools.description: Runs every enabled TypeScript code-quality check on a single source file and returns one combined report. Flags anti-patterns including any, enums, @ts-ignore, type assertions, non-null assertions, unconstrained generics, and the Function/Object/{} types. Use this whenever generating, editing, or reviewing TypeScript code. Pass the source as content and the file's absolute path as originalPath.

content.description: The TypeScript source code to check.

originalPath.description: Absolute path of the file whose source is in content.
